---
title: "Have to Change Resolution on Reboot"
date: 2009-05-27
forum: Desktop Environments
---

### Post by madverb on 2009-05-27
**xorg.conf**
```
# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@crested)  Sun Feb  1 20:25:37 UTC 2009

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    FontPath        "unix/:7100"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "BenQ G2412HD"
    HorizSync       24.0 - 83.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 76.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 8600 GT"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "metamodes" "nvidia-auto-select +0+0; 1920x1080 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection
```
When I start X it comes up in 1280x1024 resolution. I can then change it to the correct resolution (1920x1080) but on restarting X it goes straight back to 1280x1024.

---

### Post by madverb on 2009-05-27
I don't understand it. There are no errors relating to the resolutions. It seems like Nvidia just all of a sudden decides to set it to 1280x1024. It's frikken weird.

### Post by madverb on 2009-05-27
Bump

### Post by orange-wedge on 2009-05-28
Have you tried running:

```
sudo nvidia-settings
```

---

### Post by madverb on 2009-05-28
No, but I modified the Main Menu link to 'gksu nvidia-settings'.
If you look at the xorg.conf you can see that it has been generated by nvidia-settings with 1920x1080 as the default metamode setting.

### Post by madverb on 2009-05-30

### Post by madverb on 2009-06-01
Where else would it be grabbing the resolution from?

---

### Post by madverb on 2009-06-01
What config file in my home directory would be causing my resolution to convert to 1280x1024? I have moved all the hidden files and folders to a separate directory and logged out and back in and the resolution comes up correctly as 1920x1080.

---

### Post by madverb on 2009-06-01
Well, figured it out after much effing about. For some reason the gnome-display-properties or xrandr was over-riding the Nvidia settings.
I went into the gnome-display-properties and selected 'No' when it asked 'It appears that your graphics driver does not support the necessary extensions to use this tool.  Do you want to use your graphics driver vendor's tool instead?'
Then I changed the setting in there and it fixed the problem. This is plain frikken stupid.
Thank you for your time... or lack there of.

---

### Post by madverb on 2009-06-01
Even better! Just removed the ~/.config/monitors.xml and now it completely ignores the gnome settings! Login is also much smoother now. This is really damn annoying.

---
